findings.issue: Changed API routes may expose sensitive operational data without explicit auth coverage.
findings.evidence: The review packet lists PATCH /api/followups and no middleware or route-level auth proof.
findings.impact: Sensitive operational data could be read or changed by an unauthenticated caller.
findings.suggested_fix: Add explicit auth/middleware coverage and a negative route test.

findings.issue: Per-send AI opt-in is not persistent across sessions.
findings.evidence: The Review Packet says OpenRouter send requires a per-send checkbox and no saved preference.
findings.impact: Users may need to re-confirm, but protected data is not sent by default.

findings.issue: Saved browser templates could include sensitive placeholders.
findings.evidence: The Review Packet says templates are stored in localStorage.
findings.impact: If templates include real protected data, the browser could persist it longer than intended.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep saved templates to non-sensitive placeholders or add a warning.
